extraction.one_line_summary: POSTELBERGER Lucas survived from 5-man breakaway that had 5+ minutes, hung on by 11 seconds over final climbs despite visibly suffering as Astana-led chase nearly caught him.
tactical_patterns: Astana led chase but lacked numbers to close final 11 seconds despite flattening road post-Côte Masseter summit with 2km remaining.
COBRELLI positioned perfectly in select chase group over tough finale climbs, won field sprint second consecutive day but team couldn't control breakaway.
Five-man break with 5+ minutes fragmented on final climbs; POSTELBERGER survival from front better than teammates' chase control.

rider_signals.POSTELBERGER Lucas: positive: Survived alone over final climbs despite visible suffering; unexpected climbing resilience for rider without recent stage win pedigree (last stage win 2017 Giro)
rider_signals.COBRELLI Sonny: positive: Won field sprint second straight day AND survived selective finale climbs; rare sprinter who can handle tough uphill finishes
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Mentioned needing to prove Tour de France leadership capability; positioned rear of field suggests lacking form for GC ambitions
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: Strong sprint at age 41 in select group; still competitive in reduced finales
